summary: North's flagship big air kite designed for maximum boost and hangtime. The 5-strut frame provides stability in strong winds while maintaining surprisingly good freeride capabilities.

sources.summary: A strong big air kite that also works well for powered freeriding. Competitive with the Rebel and XR7.
Great for big air enthusiasts who also want to freeride. Reliable relaunch and good wind range.
A solid performer in the big air category. Riders appreciate the balance between boost and usability.

structured_review.summary: The GOAT that changed big air forever. Five years of iteration have made the Orbit incredibly refined - light bar pressure, fast turning, and that legendary catch. But it lacks the lift and second lift of newer Aluula kites, and the sweetspot moves when overpowered. Still the best value proposition in big air.
pros: Legendary fast catch - will never drop you on 8m and smaller
Lightest bar pressure with precise handling via fixed bridle
Five struts give excellent canopy rigidity and 8 angles of attack
Remarkable build quality and durability at competitive price
Changed the sport - made big air accessible to weekend warriors
Best value big air kite, especially used
cons: Lacks lift compared to Aluula generation kites
Poor second lift - does not fly behind you easily
Sweetspot moves out of comfortable range when overpowered
Fixed bridle limits maximum height potential vs pulley kites
Has plateaued - no significant innovation since 2021
structured_review.best_for: Anyone getting into big air who wants proven reliability, easy looping, and great value
structured_review.not_for: Riders chasing WOO records or maximum second lift for new school tricks
structured_review.rec_blurb: The GOAT of big air. Nothing loops easier or catches faster, and nothing comes close on value.
